cases.title: Facts dropped across separate replies are connected by one aggregate query

seed.statement: The user is from Saint Petersburg.

turns.prompt: by the way, I grew up in Piter
these days I'm over at Yandex
what do you know about me?

cases.note: The user scatters three facts across three natural-language replies ('grew up in Piter', 'over at Yandex', 'turned thirty-two'). A push engine does not self-trigger on the user's own statements — those facts are what the store already holds (the seed) — so the stating turns stay silent. The final open question ('what do you know about me?') carries no value, only a data-type meta-cue, and must connect all three scattered facts at once.

cases.title: A later correction overrides an earlier scattered fact

seed.statement: Earlier the user said they live in Moscow.
Correction: the user actually lives in Saint Petersburg now.

turns.prompt: which city do I live in now?

cases.note: Two scattered mentions of the user's city partially contradict each other; the later one is a correction (declares supersedes). The query must return only the current city and never surface both — otherwise the agent sees Moscow and Saint Petersburg side by side and cannot tell which is live.
